channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Texas Republicans nominate Ken Paxton for Senate seat, ousting incumbent John Cornyn
item.description: Controversial Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton challenged Sen. John Cornyn's reelection and won President Trump's endorsement in the bitter primary fight that cost Republicans more than $100 million.
item.content:encoded:
Controversial Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton challenged Sen. John Cornyn's reelection and won President Trump's endorsement in the bitter primary fight that cost Republicans more than $100 million.

(Image credit: Sergio Flores)
item.dc:creator: Claudia Grisales

item.title: Researchers are building AI-powered robot labs. What does this mean for science?
item.description: Thanks to new technologies like artificial intelligence, scientists are increasingly freed from the constraints of the laboratory. It raises questions about how much humans should outsource to robots.
item.content:encoded: Thanks to new technologies like artificial intelligence, scientists are increasingly freed from the constraints of the laboratory. It raises questions about how much humans should outsource to robots.
item.dc:creator: Katia Riddle

item.title: 39 World Cup teams will be based in the U.S. Here's which squad will be closest to you
item.description: This week, FIFA finalized the list of where competitors will train during the tournament. Here's a breakdown of the U.S. cities that are included.
item.content:encoded:
This week, FIFA finalized the list of where competitors will train during the tournament. Here's a breakdown of the U.S. cities that are included.

(Image credit: Dustin Satloff)
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: To stop leaks, the Trump administration wants federal workers to sign NDAs
item.description: The Trump administration has proposed creating a new government-wide nondisclosure agreement for new and existing federal employees.
item.content:encoded:
The Trump administration has proposed creating a new government-wide nondisclosure agreement for new and existing federal employees.

(Image credit: Michael A. McCoy)
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu

item.title: Gulf shrimpers want help from Congress as fuel costs climb
item.description: The declining number of Gulf shrimpers who are still in business are now struggling as gas prices rise and competition with cheaper imports remains high.
item.content:encoded:
The declining number of Gulf shrimpers who are still in business are now struggling as gas prices rise and competition with cheaper imports remains high.

(Image credit: Jay Marcano for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Drew Hawkins

item.title: Trump-backed redistricting plan is rejected in the South Carolina Legislature
item.description: Republican state senators don't face election this year. Trump's urging for them to redistrict to help flip the House seat held by prominent Democrat Jim Clyburn was met with opposition.
item.content:encoded:
Republican state senators don't face election this year. Trump's urging for them to redistrict to help flip the House seat held by prominent Democrat Jim Clyburn was met with opposition.

(Image credit: Jeffrey Collins)
item.dc:creator: Gavin Jackson

item.title: Trump DOJ mass-deletes info on Jan. 6 riot cases, including violent assaults on cops
item.description: The Trump Department of Justice purged government news releases with information about prosecutions of rioters who attacked the U.S. Capitol and assaulted law enforcement on Jan. 6, 2021.
item.content:encoded:
The Trump Department of Justice purged government news releases with information about prosecutions of rioters who attacked the U.S. Capitol and assaulted law enforcement on Jan. 6, 2021.

(Image credit: Joseph Prezioso)
item.dc:creator: Tom Dreisbach

item.title: A mix of veterans and newcomers will represent the United States at the World Cup
item.description: Some veterans from the last World Cup — like forward Christian Pulisic and midfielders Weston McKennie and Tyler Adams — made the team. The roster includes 13 newcomers as well.
item.content:encoded:
Some veterans from the last World Cup — like forward Christian Pulisic and midfielders Weston McKennie and Tyler Adams — made the team. The roster includes 13 newcomers as well.

(Image credit: Adam Hunger)
item.dc:creator: Becky Sullivan

item.title: Why the French Open is named after Roland Garros, who didn't play tennis
item.description: Roland Garros, a WWI-era pilot, is credited with revolutionizing aerial combat. The major Paris tennis tournament was named after him in 1928, a decade after his death in combat.
item.content:encoded:
Roland Garros, a WWI-era pilot, is credited with revolutionizing aerial combat. The major Paris tennis tournament was named after him in 1928, a decade after his death in combat.

(Image credit: Branger/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: An exceptionally early heat wave shatters records and brings deaths in Europe
item.description: Temperature records are breaking and triggering government warnings, with London hitting 95 degrees the past two days. Experts say unpredictable and extreme weather is becoming more frequent.
item.content:encoded:
Temperature records are breaking and triggering government warnings, with London hitting 95 degrees the past two days. Experts say unpredictable and extreme weather is becoming more frequent.

(Image credit: Kin Cheung)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
